before: With Bunk & Bechet in Boston (1999), clarinetist Sidney Bechet delivers a dixieland program for the Jazz Crusade Records label. before: On Capricorn Rising, tenor saxophonist Sam Rivers leads a free jazz session recorded for Black Saint in 1993. Among the tracks are "Break Out" and "Capricorn Rising". The album comes from the contemporary era's stylistic eclecticism, drawing on the full history of jazz and beyond. Though most famously a tenor saxophonist, he also performed on soprano saxophone, bass clarinet, flute, harmonica, piano and viola.
after: On Capricorn Rising, tenor saxophonist Sam Rivers leads a free jazz session recorded for Black Saint in 1976. Among the tracks are "Break Out" and "Capricorn Rising". The album comes from the contemporary era's stylistic eclecticism, drawing on the full history of jazz and beyond. Though most famously a tenor saxophonist, he also performed on soprano saxophone, bass clarinet, flute, harmonica, piano and viola.
evidenceUsed: MusicBrainz date 1976 (the 1993 year was wrong); Wikipedia Sam Rivers page (entityMatch=yes) supports the multi-instrument sentence. Corrected year only, rest preserved verbatim.

before: South African pianist and multi-instrumentalist Bheki Mseleku's triumphant debut, featuring Pharoah Sanders, Joe Henderson, and Elvin Jones. The album radiates spiritual warmth, combining Coltrane-influenced modal jazz with the melodic richness of South African church music. Introduced Mseleku to the international jazz world and demonstrated the continued vitality of the South African jazz tradition. His ability to unite American and African jazz masters was remarkable.
after: Celebration is the 1992 debut album by South African musician Bheki Mseleku, released on World Circuit. Recorded in two days, it was shortlisted for the 1992 Mercury Prize, with a contribution from Courtney Pine. The program includes "Celebration," "Angola," "One for All - All for One," and "Blues for Afrika."
evidenceUsed: Rewrote to evidence-only: Wikipedia (entityMatch=yes: debut, 1992 Mercury Prize shortlist, recorded in two days, Courtney Pine) + MusicBrainz (1992, World Circuit, tracklist). Dropped the unverifiable personnel claim (Pharoah Sanders/Joe Henderson/Elvin Jones) — not in any entity-matched evidence.

before: Chesed (The Book Beriah Volume 4) is a 2018 album by composer and saxophonist John Zorn, released on Tzadik. Part of Zorn's Book Beriah series exploring the ten sefirot of Kabbalistic tradition, the album features his Masada ensemble navigating the intersection of Jewish musical themes and avant-garde jazz. From the contemporary era's stylistic eclecticism, drawing on the full history of jazz and beyond, this album positions Julian Lage within the avant-garde jazz conversation of the time.
after: Chesed: The Book Beri'ah, Volume 4 is a 2019 album of compositions by John Zorn, released on Tzadik and performed by guitarists Julian Lage and Gyan Riley. Part of Zorn's Book Beri'ah series exploring the ten sefirot of Kabbalistic tradition, the duo navigates the intersection of Jewish musical themes and avant-garde jazz. From the contemporary era's stylistic eclecticism, drawing on the full history of jazz and beyond, this album positions Julian Lage within the avant-garde jazz conversation of the time.
evidenceUsed: MusicBrainz: date 2019-04 (corrects '2018'), credited performers Julian Lage + Gyan Riley (corrects 'his Masada ensemble'), Tzadik. Wikipedia Julian Lage page (entityMatch=yes) confirms Lage and Gyan Riley are guitarists. Both flagged claims fixed.

before: Benny Goodman's Clarinet Concertos Nos. 1 And 2, cut for RCA Red Seal in 1968, stands as a distinguished entry in the swing canon. The set list features 'Concerto for Clarinet and Orchestra no. 1 in F minor, op. 73: I. Allegro' and 'Concerto for Clarinet and Orchestra no. 1 in F minor, op. 73: II. Adagio ma non troppo' among its standout moments.
after: Benny Goodman's Clarinet Concertos Nos. 1 And 2, cut for RCA Red Seal in 1968, is a classical recording of Weber's two clarinet concertos performed with the Chicago Symphony under conductor Jean Martinon. The set list features 'Concerto for Clarinet and Orchestra no. 1 in F minor, op. 73: I. Allegro' and 'Concerto for Clarinet and Orchestra no. 1 in F minor, op. 73: II. Adagio ma non troppo' among its standout moments.
evidenceUsed: MusicBrainz artists (Weber, Chicago Symphony, Jean Martinon) and classical-movement tracklist show this is a Weber clarinet-concerto recording, not swing; corrected only the genre misdescription, second sentence preserved verbatim.
